page: Amazon Web Services r8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 192 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Neoverse-V2 ARM64 architecture running at 2.7 GHz. It features 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor cache configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this server, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth. The stress-ng multi-core test, PassMark memory score, and memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks rank in the top 10% of all tested hardware. Database operations and Redis SET operations also achieve top-tier status, with Redis reaching 357.86 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the middle 50%. Additionally, while prompt processing for medium and large language models is strong, text generation speeds for both small and large models are weak, falling into the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8g.metal-48xl offers high resource density and memory-to-vCPU ratios without the overhead of virtualization, making it highly efficient for memory-bound workloads. The lack of local storage requires external network storage integration, which is a key architectural tradeoff. Given its massive DDR5 memory capacity, high multi-core throughput, and top-tier database performance, this server is designed for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis caching layers, heavy database operations, and high-throughput static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r8g.metal-48xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 AWS Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.7 GHz. Equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory, it delivers 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds remain average to weak. This hardware profile is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, making it suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis caching, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.metal-48xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server with 192 ARM64 Neoverse-V2 cores, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier database and multi-core performance.

tagline: A bare-metal ARM64 server featuring 192 dedicated cores and 1.5 TB of DDR5 memory for memory-intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated physical ARM64 Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.7 GHz
1536.0 GB of high-bandwidth DDR5 memory (8.0 GB per core)
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Suited for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis caching, and web serving
